title: WorktreeState struct is dead YAGNI scaffolding never instantiated
description:
## Severity
Trivial

## Dimension
YAGNI / Documentation & Intent

## Location
src/tools/worktree.rs:48-54 (WorktreeState struct)

## Violation
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct WorktreeState {
    pub path: PathBuf,
    pub branch: String,
    pub original_cwd: PathBuf,
}
```

`grep -rn WorktreeState src/` returns only this definition — the struct is never instantiated or referenced. It violates YAGNI: speculative scaffolding kept "for future use." Worse, its `original_cwd` field implies a restore-on-exit semantic that the actual exit_worktree implementation does not honor (exit_worktree changes cwd to `main_path = common_dir.parent()`, never to `original_cwd`).

## Mandated refactor
Delete the struct. If the future use becomes a present need, recreate it with fields that match actual behavior.

## Filed from QA review dispatch
